response.reasoning:
Looking at the static analysis output, I notice a key issue: 3 of the 4 changed files have been placed in the infrastructure group because they are "not reachable from any detected entrypoint." However, the branch is named "feature/reexport" and the files involved are:

- src/adapter/index.ts
- src/core/dataService.ts
- src/facade/index.ts

These names — `adapter/index.ts` and `facade/index.ts` — are strongly suggestive of re-export barrel files, which is entirely consistent with the branch name `feature/reexport`. Re-export/barrel index files are commonly added as new module surface area and would NOT be reachable from existing entrypoints precisely because they ARE the new exports being introduced. They aren't "unreachable infrastructure" — they are the core logical change of this PR.

Furthermore, `src/core/dataService.ts` is the underlying service being exposed, and `src/routes/data.ts` is likely being updated to use or expose it. Together, all four files form a cohesive feature: introducing a re-export pattern through a facade/adapter layer backed by a dataService.

The static analysis correctly identified `src/routes/data.ts` as an entrypoint but the symbol graph couldn't follow the new re-export chain (likely because the symbols didn't exist before this diff). The infrastructure group classification is misleading — these files are central to the feature, not peripheral infrastructure.

The best refinement here is to reclassify the three infrastructure files into group_1 (the route flow group), since they are all part of the same "feature/reexport" logical change. Alternatively, since group_1 has 0 additions/deletions in data.ts (it may just be a touch/import update), these files could form their own group — but merging into the single feature group is more appropriate given the small scope (4 files total).

Actually, re-reading: `src/routes/data.ts` has 0 additions and 0 deletions listed, which is suspicious. It may have been detected as an entrypoint due to existing code, but the real changes are in the three infrastructure-classified files. Given the branch name "feature/reexport", the three files (adapter/index.ts, facade/index.ts, core/dataService.ts) represent the new re-export layer being built. They should be reviewed together as the primary change, not buried in the infrastructure group.

I'll reclassify all three infrastructure files into group_1 so reviewers see the full picture of this re-export feature in one cohesive group.
